name: Thalia Grace
description: Thalia Grace, daughter of Zeus and the mortal Beryl Grace, is a powerful demigoddess within Greek mythology, albeit a more modern interpretation stemming from contemporary literature. Her story blends classical themes with modern narratives of identity, sacrifice, and destiny. Thalia is primarily known for her role as the guardian of Zeus's magical Pine Tree on Half-Blood Hill, a sacred space that protects Camp Half-Blood from monsters. Her transformation into a pine tree was a selfless act to save her friends, Annabeth Chase, Luke Castellan, and Grover Underwood, from a horde of monsters. This sacrifice cemented her legend and demonstrated her immense bravery and loyalty. After several years, she was revived from the tree, regaining her human form and reclaiming her demigod status. Thalia is a skilled warrior, inheriting her father's powers over the sky and storms. She can manipulate electricity, summon lightning, and control the weather to a degree. Her weapon of choice is a magical Aegis bracelet, which transforms into a shield bearing the head of Medusa, capable of petrifying enemies. Thalia eventually joins the Hunters of Artemis, swearing off romantic relationships and dedicating herself to the goddess of the hunt. This decision allows her to avoid the prophecy surrounding the children of the Big Three (Zeus, Poseidon, and Hades), offering her a path to immortality and freedom from the potential for great destruction or being used by Kronos. Her fierce independence, unwavering loyalty, and connection to Zeus make her a significant figure in the demigod world. She represents strength, resilience, and the ability to forge one's own destiny even amidst the challenges of divine parentage and ancient prophecies. Thalia's story is about choosing one’s own path and facing impossible odds with courage and conviction.
subtitle: Daughter of Zeus, Hunter of Artemis

keyMyths.title: Transformation into a Pine Tree
keyMyths.description: Thalia sacrificed herself to protect her friends, Annabeth, Luke, and Grover, from monsters. Zeus, honoring her bravery, transformed her into a magical pine tree that served as a protective barrier for Camp Half-Blood.
keyMyths.source: Percy Jackson and the Lightning Thief

keyMyths.title: Joining the Hunters of Artemis
keyMyths.description: To avoid the prophecy surrounding the children of the Big Three, Thalia joined the Hunters of Artemis. This granted her immortality and allowed her to dedicate herself to the hunt, free from the constraints of destiny.
keyMyths.source: Percy Jackson and the Titan's Curse

keyMyths.title: Control over Storms
keyMyths.description: As a daughter of Zeus, Thalia possesses the power to manipulate storms and control lightning. She can summon lightning bolts and use her powers to defend herself and her allies in battle.

sources.author: Rick Riordan
sources.description: The primary source for Thalia Grace's character and mythology.
sources.text: The primary source for Thalia Grace's character and mythology.
